prompt_sections.premise: Create a story in my established writing style about Dane, a software engineer who discovers his stock prediction algorithm points to a catastrophic market crash.
prompt_sections.storylines:
Corporate pressure around Dane's presentation.
The uncertainty over whether the model is wrong or prophetic.
Intersecting finance, office politics, and personal strain.
prompt_sections.setting: A contemporary financial technology company in a modern city, with offices, transit, and corporate presentation rooms.
prompt_sections.characters:
Dane: brilliant, overworked software engineer.
Gary: nervous boss trying to balance support and optics.
Jonathan Morego: skeptical executive asking hard questions.
prompt_sections.writing_style: Third-person limited focused on Dane. Direct and technical around code and markets, more introspective in personal moments.
prompt_sections.tone: Technical thriller blended with workplace drama, grounded and tense.
prompt_sections.plot_beats:
Dane finishes the model late at night.
The model predicts a crash.
He oversleeps and races to the presentation.
Executives challenge the validity of his findings.
prompt_sections.constraints:
Use intersecting storylines. Keep the corporate and financial details grounded in reality. Maintain tension around whether the prediction is an error.
Around 1000 words per chapter.
